# PumpKit — Full Technical Reference

> Open-source TypeScript framework for real-time PumpFun monitoring on Solana.

This document provides comprehensive technical information about PumpKit for AI systems, search engines, and developers.

---

## Overview

PumpKit is a modular, open-source TypeScript framework that monitors pump.fun on-chain activity on the Solana blockchain. It detects events in real time — fee claims, token launches, whale trades, graduations, CTO (community takeover) events — enriches them with off-chain data (GitHub profiles, X/Twitter metadata), and delivers alerts through multiple channels including Telegram, Discord, Slack, webhooks, and a live web dashboard.

## Architecture

```
Solana RPC (WebSocket + HTTP)
    │
    ▼
┌──────────────┐
│ @pumpkit/core │  ← Solana monitors, decoders, storage, logging
└──────┬───────┘
       │
  ┌────┴────┬──────────┬──────────┐
  ▼         ▼          ▼          ▼
monitor   channel    claim     tracker
  │         │          │          │
  ▼         ▼          ▼          ▼
REST API  Telegram   Telegram  Telegram
SSE       broadcast   bot       bot
Webhooks
Dashboard
```

## Event Types

### Token Launch (`launch`)
Detected when a new token is created on pump.fun.
- Fields: mint address, token name, symbol, description, creator wallet, social links (Twitter, Telegram, website), GitHub handle, creation timestamp
- Enrichment: GitHub profile data, X follower count, cashback badge, mayhem badge

### Trade Alert (`trade`)
Detected when a buy or sell exceeds the configured whale threshold.
- Fields: mint, trader wallet, direction (buy/sell), SOL amount, token amount, market cap, bonding curve progress %, creator fee info
- Configurable whale threshold (default: 1 SOL)

### Graduation (`graduation`)
Detected when a token migrates from pump.fun bonding curve to Raydium AMM.
- Fields: mint, Raydium pool address, migration fee, final market cap, total supply

### Fee Claim (`claim`)
Detected when a creator claims accrued social fees.
- Fields: mint, claimer wallet, SOL amount claimed, GitHub handle, lifetime claim total, claim count

### Fee Distribution (`distribution`)
Shows how fees are split across shareholders.
- Fields: mint, shareholders list with wallet addresses and BPS (basis points) allocations

### CTO Alert (`cto`)
Community takeover detection — triggered when the original creator sells a significant portion.
- Fields: mint, creator wallet, sell amount, remaining balance

## Packages — Detailed

### @pumpkit/core v0.3.2
The shared foundation package.

**Bot Scaffolding**
- Grammy-based Telegram bot with middleware router
- Command registration with help text generation
- Rate limiting and error handling middleware

**Solana Monitoring**
- WebSocket subscription for real-time transaction monitoring
- HTTP polling fallback with configurable interval
- Automatic RPC endpoint failover (multiple endpoints)
- Transaction parsing for pump.fun and Raydium program instructions

**Program Decoders**
- pump.fun program account decoder (token metadata, bonding curve state)
- Raydium AMM decoder (pool state, migration events)

**Storage**
- File-based JSON storage (default)
- SQLite adapter for production
- Claim history tracking with aggregation

**Utilities**
- Structured logging via pino
- Health-check HTTP server (GET /health)
- SOL/lamports formatting
- Wallet address truncation
- Markdown template rendering

### @pumpkit/monitor v1.0.0
The all-in-one monitoring service.

**REST API Endpoints**
- `GET /api/events` — paginated event history
- `GET /api/events/stream` — SSE real-time event stream
- `GET /api/claims/:mint` — claim history for a token
- `GET /api/watches` — list watched tokens/wallets
- `POST /api/watches` — add a watch
- `DELETE /api/watches/:id` — remove a watch
- `GET /api/stats` — aggregate statistics
- `GET /health` — health check

**SSE Event Stream**
Server-Sent Events stream delivering real-time events to the web dashboard and external consumers. Events are JSON-encoded with type discrimination.

**Webhook Support**
- POST webhooks to Discord, Slack, or custom endpoints
- Configurable event type filtering per webhook
- Retry with exponential backoff

**Configuration**
- `SOLANA_RPC_URL` — Primary Solana RPC endpoint
- `SOLANA_RPC_FALLBACK` — Fallback RPC endpoints (comma-separated)
- `WHALE_THRESHOLD` — Minimum SOL for whale alerts (default: 1)
- `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` — Telegram bot API token
- `TELEGRAM_CHANNEL_ID` — Target Telegram channel
- `GROQ_API_KEY` — Optional Groq LLM for enrichment
- `GITHUB_TOKEN` — GitHub API token for profile enrichment

### @pumpkit/channel v0.2.1
Read-only Telegram channel broadcaster.

- Zero interaction design — no commands, pure broadcast
- Customizable Markdown templates per event type
- Event type filtering (include/exclude)
- Token CA filtering (watch specific tokens)
- Rate limiting with priority queue (Telegram API limits)

### @pumpkit/claim v0.1.8
Interactive Telegram bot for fee claim lookups.

**Commands**
- `/claim <CA>` — Show all fee claims for a token contract address
- `/creator <handle>` — Show all claims by a GitHub creator handle
- `/leaderboard` — Top claimers by lifetime SOL
- `/export <CA>` — Export claims as CSV

### @pumpkit/tracker v0.1.0
Personal watchlist Telegram bot.

**Commands**
- `/watch <address>` — Watch a wallet or token mint
- `/unwatch <address>` — Remove from watchlist
- `/list` — Show current watchlist
- `/alerts` — Configure alert thresholds

**Features**
- Per-user watchlists
- Threshold-crossing trade alerts
- Creator sell notifications
- Graduation alerts for watched tokens

## Web Dashboard

Built with React 18, Vite, Tailwind CSS, and React Router v6.

**Pages**
- `/` — Landing page with feature overview
- `/dashboard` — Real-time event feed with SSE connection, filtering, search, and statistics
- `/create` — Token creation demo/tutorial
- `/docs` — Interactive documentation (Getting Started, Tutorials, Architecture, Packages, Bot Commands, API, FAQ)
- `/packages` — Package showcase with features and download stats

**Tech Stack**
- React 18 with TypeScript
- Vite 6 build tool
- Tailwind CSS 3 styling
- React Router v6 client-side routing
- Server-Sent Events for real-time data
- Telegram-inspired UI design

## Deployment

**Docker**
```dockerfile
FROM node:20-slim
# Multi-stage build, production-optimized
```

**Railway**
One-click deploy with `railway.json` configuration.

**Environment Variables**
See @pumpkit/monitor configuration section above.
